source: CT6-6 requirements §11 (HD-1 … HD-3) + §9 EVAL-15/16/17 privacy: a manual triage-submission skill (Logit/Helpdesk) the user runs after a bad session — component 5 of the in-repo tier

scenarios: a consented, versioned submission is built and validates
acceptance_criteria: build_submission requires consent + version; stamps version + source:manual-helpdesk (same triage process)

scenarios: the skill + command document the manual consent flow
summary strips unlisted identifiable content and off produces nothing
acceptance_criteria: summary is an allow-list (default-deny): drops code/data/nested/unknown/non-dict (EVAL-16)
validate_submission rejects non-allow-listed keys under summary (backstop)
server-tier send boundary (SEC + EVAL) stated in skill + command; never claim 'sent' for a local-only payload
privacy levels + allow-list leak regressions (unlisted/nested/non-dict) + consent/version guards
full suite green cp1252 AND PYTHONUTF8=1

testing_authorizations.playwright: N/A — no UI/frontend surface (a stdlib-only engine + a skill + a command + tests + docs).
testing_authorizations.dev_api_integration: N/A — the actual triage-server send is the server-tier (not in-repo); the engine + CLI are exercised by the pytest suite (incl. real subprocess round-trip).
testing_authorizations.both_layer_front_to_back: N/A — single-layer (Python engine + skill/command doctrine) change.
